problem.title: Longest Consecutive Sequence
problem.description: Given an unsorted array of integers nums, return the length of the longest consecutive elements sequence.
assumptions: Sequence values must be consecutive integers.

example.explanation: Consecutive sequence: 1,2,3,4 has length 4. Use hash set for O(n).

solutions.title: Solution 1 – HashSet
solutions.description: Insert all numbers into a hash set for O(1) lookups, then iterate over unique values. A sequence is only started from a number that has no left neighbor (num-1 not in set), which avoids redundant work. Expanding each sequence rightward gives O(n) total time despite the nested loop.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import List

class Solution:
    def longestConsecutive(self, nums: List[int]) -> int:
        num_set = set(nums)
        max_length = 0

        for num in num_set:
            if num - 1 not in num_set:  # start of a sequence
                current_num = num
                current_length = 1

                while current_num + 1 in num_set:
                    current_num += 1
                    current_length += 1

                max_length = max(max_length, current_length)

        return max_length

solutions.title: Solution 1 – HashSet
solutions.description: Insert all numbers into a hash set for O(1) lookups, then iterate over unique values. A sequence is only started from a number that has no left neighbor (num-1 not in set), which avoids redundant work. Expanding each sequence rightward gives O(n) total time despite the nested loop.
